title: Anthephora cristata
taxonomy: Anthephora cristata is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Aristidoideae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis. The genus Anthephora is a part of the tribe Aristideae. The species is further categorized as a type of grama grass.

intro: Anthephora cristata, also known as Crested Wheatgrass, is a perennial grass native to the dry, grassy plains of southern Africa. Traditionally, Anthephora cristata has been used to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Anthephora cristata is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat digestive issues and promote relaxation.
